b:T17062,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. change to conformation/shape/tertiary structure/3-D shape;<br/>b. bonds within the protein/intramolecular bonds broken/changed;<br/>c. pH and temperature (outside tolerated ranges) can cause denaturation;<br/>d. vibrations/heat at high temperatures breaks bonds;<br/>e. high pH/low pH/extreme pH alters ionization/charges (of amino acids and breaks ionic bonds);<br/>f. protein cannot carry out its function<br/><strong>OR</strong><br/><span style="text-decoration:underline;">active site</span> of <span style="text-decoration:underline;">enzymes</span> cannot bind substrates/catalyze reaction/no enzyme-substate complex;<br/>g. permanent/irreversible change (usually)<br/><strong>OR</strong><br/>soluble proteins become insoluble/precipitate;</p>
<p><em>Allow any mark points if made clearly on an annotated graph or diagram</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. antigens stimulate antibody production;<br/>b. antibodies produced by lymphocytes;<br/>c. macrophages/phagocytes ingest/engulf pathogens and display antigens from them;<br/>d. T-cells activated by binding antigen/by macrophage displaying antigen;<br/>e. activated T-cells cause activation of B-cells;<br/>f. mitosis/division of (activated) B-cells (to produce a clone of cells)<br/>g. plasma cells formed from divided/activated/growing/differentiating B-cells;<br/>h. plasma cells/plasma B-cells secrete antibodies;<br/>i. clonal selection / plasma cells make same type of antibody/antibody specific to same antigen;<br/>j. some activated B-cells become memory cells;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p><em>Accept mpd in a graph</em>.</p>
<p><em>Accept mpa, mpb or mpc on an annotated diagram</em>.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="display:block;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;"/></p>
<p style="text-align:center;">[Source: Enzyme inhibition curves,<br/>ImranKhan1992, Available at: <a href="https://%20commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png#/media/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png">https://</a><br/><a href="https://%20commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png#/media/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png">commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/</a><br/><a href="https://%20commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png#/media/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png">File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png#/media/</a><br/><a href="https://%20commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png#/media/%20File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png">File:Enzyme_kinetics_curve.png</a> CC0 1.0<br/>Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication<br/><a href="https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/%20zero/1.0/deed.en">https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/</a><br/><a href="https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/%20zero/1.0/deed.en">zero/1.0/deed.en</a> Source adapted.]</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>c:T73a,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Most candidates knew that denaturation can be caused by excessive temperatures or extreme pH. Fewer candidates mentioned how bonds within a protein are broken by heat or kinetic energy. Even fewer explained how R-group ionization in acidic and basic amino acids can be altered by pH changes, breaking the ionic bonds within protein molecules that help to stabilize tertiary structure.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>This was another question giving scope to candidates to show the breadth of their biological understanding. The best answers were impressive but given the challenging nature of the topic it isn’t surprising that there were also some very muddled accounts. The weakest candidates struggled to remember the differences between antigens, antibodies and lymphocytes.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>In a parallel way to 6(c) and 7(b), there was a tendency for candidates to write about the two types of enzyme inhibition separately and as a result not to distinguish between them fully. For example. many candidates stated that competitive inhibitors show some chemical similarity to the substrate but did not then state that non-competitive inhibitors are typically dissimilar. An error which is repeatedly seen in enzyme questions is for candidates to state that the active site is on the substrate rather than the enzyme. Some candidates drew sketch graphs to show the difference between competitive and non-competitive inhibitors in the effect of substrate concentration, but either did not label the x-axis or labelled it ‘time’ rather than ‘substrate concentration’.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/2/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","2"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"21N.2.HL.TZ0.8"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"21N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","2-4-proteins, 11-1-antibody-production-and-vaccination, 8-1-metabolism"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"specification\">\n<p>Proteins carry out many important functions in cells and in organisms.</p>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;\">\n<p>Outline the process of protein denaturation.</p>\n<div class=\"marks\">[4]</div>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;\">\n<p>Explain the production of antibodies in humans.</p>\n<div class=\"marks\">[7]</div>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;\">\n<p>Distinguish between competitive and non-competitive enzyme inhibition.</p>\n<div class=\"marks\">[4]</div>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}]]}]
